channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Quantum Compositional NLP for Arabic: Grammar, Morphology, and Word Sense in Circuit Topology
item.description: arXiv:2607.14100v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present the first application of pregroup grammar-based quantum compositional natural language processing (QNLP) to Arabic; a morphologically rich, free-word-order language whose structural complexity provides a uniquely demanding testbed for theor...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, experiment, embedding, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Latent Communication Between Language Model Agents: Channels, Alignment, and the Limits of Text
item.description: arXiv:2607.14103v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent systems (MAS) are utilized in many contexts and many professions. Those MAS rely on inter-agent communication, usually implemented by clear-text message passing. We hypothesize that Large Language Models may have a world model at their dis...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, analysis, retrieval, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Automatically Evolving Prompt Guidelines for Task-Specific Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2607.14105v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: For Large Language Models to reliably answer user queries, users must clearly specify requirements, context, and constraints. In practice, however, user queries are often underspecified, forcing models to infer unstated assumptions that may misalign w...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, RAG, study, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Eta Given Delta: Defining LLM Tool Efficiency With Marginal Tool Utility
item.description: arXiv:2607.14108v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper introduces tool efficiency, a new quantitative metric to evaluate the rate of useful tool calls in an LLM agent trajectory. To ensure that tool efficiency is well-defined, we also introduce marginal tool utility, a new quantitative metric d...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, paper, tool, arxiv, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: IMEX Interaction-Based Model Explanation
item.description: arXiv:2607.14096v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In predictive modeling, the ability to explain why a model produces a given target prediction has become increasingly important [5, 10]. Black-box models do not provide a transparent description of the internal mechanisms that generate the prediction,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, analysis, experiment, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DialogueVPR: Towards Conversational Visual Place Recognition
item.description: arXiv:2607.14115v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Inspired by how humans communicate spatial information, language-guided geo-localization has gained significant traction for its intuitive and practical value. Despite this progress, most methods still rely on a static, one-shot retrieval paradigm, wh...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, retrieval, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Interpretable Language Model for Closed-Loop Type 1 Diabetes Control
item.description: arXiv:2607.14126v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Type 1 Diabetes (T1D) is a chronic, life-threatening autoimmune condition characterized by the complete destruction of insulin-producing pancreatic beta cells. While Artificial Pancreas Systems (APS) powered by Reinforcement Learning (RL) have shown p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, paper, arxiv, large language model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Capability from Access Structure, Not Scale: Lower Bounds and Pre-Registered Tests for Hybrid Sequence Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.14144v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Platonic Representation Hypothesis (PRH) holds that as models scale, representations of heterogeneous networks converge toward a shared model of reality. We propose its sequel and boundary, the Capability Convergence Hypothesis (CCH): under a fixe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, attention, retrieval, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Low-Latency Relay Selection in NR-V2X Vehicular Communications via Graph Isomorphism Networks with Edge Features
item.description: arXiv:2607.14176v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reliable, low-latency uplink connectivity is a key requirement for C-V2X networks in dense urban environments, where fast channel variations and blockages often degrade direct vehicle-to-infrastructure links. Multi-hop relaying can restore coverage, b...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, ICL, framework, paper, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Closed-Loop Knowledge Dynamics: An Operational Framework for Saturation and Escape
item.description: arXiv:2607.14185v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Feedback-driven loops support iterative improvement in large language models, reinforcement learning, and autonomous discovery, yet their gains often diminish under repeated internal feedback. We study why closed-loop knowledge systems saturate and wh...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, analysis, tool, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LBA: Textual Hard-Label Adversarial Attack under Low Query Budgets
item.description: arXiv:2607.14101v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generating high-quality adversarial texts with low query budgets remains a challenging problem in the hard-label scenario. Most existing approaches rely on greedy algorithms, where one position in the text is selected for substitution, followed by the...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, example, arxiv, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Position: Explainability Research Must Prioritize Foundations over Ad-hoc Methods
item.description: arXiv:2607.14123v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the proliferation of Explainable AI (XAI) techniques -- from feature attributions to sparse autoencoders -- explanations rarely influence real-world workflows. In practice, they are often generated and discarded without guiding meaningful acti...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, research, analysis, paper, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: QFireNet: A Quantum-Enhanced U-Net for Wildfire Segmentation from Sentinel-2 Imagery
item.description: arXiv:2607.14160v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Wildfire detection from satellite imagery is a semantic image segmentation problem that has proven to be difficult due to challenges such as class imbalance, feature complexity, and atmospheric interference. In this paper, we build on the foundational...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, paper, analysis, experiment, image | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Intelligent Three Level Learning Architecture for Autonomous UAV Swarms in Search and Rescue
item.description: arXiv:2607.14093v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents a novel three level hierarchical learning architecture for autonomous UAV swarms performing search and rescue operations. Unlike conventional approaches that apply a single learning paradigm across all hierarchy levels, the propose...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, analysis, paper, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: UzWordnet and Generative AI for Learning Uzbek by Game Playing
item.description: arXiv:2607.14104v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents an educational system architecture that enables learners to practice the Uzbek language through game-playing. The architecture integrates UzWordnet and the largest currently available orthographic dictionary for Uzbek as core lexic...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, product, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Token Time Continuous Diffusion for Language Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2607.14106v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In this paper we introduce token time continuous diffusion (TTCD), a new diffusion language model which (a) operates in continuous space, deterministically mapping Gaussian noise to a final token canvas with no further sampling, and crucially (b) inco...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Explainable Geospatial AI for Satellite Ground Station Siting Using LiDAR-Derived Terrain Intelligence
item.description: arXiv:2607.14127v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Representative clutter height (RCH) is a key parameter in radio propagation and interference analysis because it captures the dominant height of local obstructions that drive terminal clutter loss. Current practice often relies on fixed clutter height...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, analysis, product, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
